findings.title: SimplePriceOracle lets any account arbitrarily reprice listed assets

best_agent_match.title: SimplePriceOracle lets any account arbitrarily reprice collateral and debt

findings.title: Rewards claims can be reentered before accrued balances are cleared

best_agent_match.title: Reward transfers can silently fail while user accrual is still cleared

findings.title: A reverting rewards distributor can brick core market actions until the comptroller is upgraded

best_agent_match.title: A single bad rewards distributor can permanently DOS mint, borrow, repay, transfer, and liquidation flows

findings.title: Unchecked reward-token transfers can erase accrued rewards without payment

best_agent_match.title: Rewards claims are reentrant and can pay the same rewards repeatedly

findings.title: Reservoir drip accounting advances even when token transfer fails silently

best_agent_match.title: Reservoir can permanently under-distribute rewards on silent transfer failures

rejected_candidates.title: Unitroller fallback can hot-swap comptroller logic on any user call
rejected_candidates.reason: The fallback does auto-switch implementations when autoImplementation is enabled, but the harmful scenario depends on privileged Fuse-admin registry behavior or unspecified future implementation migration requirements; this is too speculative as a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: A hardcoded external Fuse admin keeps hidden superuser control over pools and markets
rejected_candidates.reason: The hardcoded Fuse admin and its rights are explicit in storage and admin checks. Absent evidence that this authority is undisclosed, this is privileged governance design rather than an unintended access-control flaw.

rejected_candidates.title: Price Oracle Manipulation Enables Arbitrary Liquidations
rejected_candidates.reason: Duplicate of the unrestricted SimplePriceOracle setters issue already captured in F-001; forced liquidations are one exploit path of the same root cause.
